title: Market Sizing — Method, Not Number
subtitle: The TAM/SAM/SOM funnel and the inputs we are missing
description: This is deliberately NOT a set of concentric circles with dollar figures. A defensible market size needs operator counts, size distributions, and willingness-to-pay data we do not yet have. This funnel shows the METHOD and marks each stage with the specific input required to fill it. A founder who shows the method and names the missing input is more credible than one who presents a fabricated number.

data.required_input: Global operator counts by system type
data.note: Do NOT use the USD 312.8bn industry output as TAM — that is the value of the FISH, not the addressable technology market. See industry-statistics.json#/how_to_use_these_statistics.

data.stage: SAM — reachable geography, suitable systems, above scale threshold
data.required_input: Philippine + SEA operator counts; the buoy-crossover scale threshold
data.note: Excludes RAS, deep offshore, and single small ponds below the threshold where a buoy wins. See market-sizing-tam-sam-som.json#/market_definition.

data.required_input: Dock network capacity, service capacity, regulatory permission, adoption rate
data.note: Constrained by dock coverage and BVLOS, not by demand.

the_blocking_inputs.note: Render these prominently — the missing inputs ARE the content of this chart.

inputs.input: Number of aquaculture operations in the Philippines by system and scale
inputs.status: NOT OBTAINED — the single most important missing datum in the repository

inputs.input: Cage counts per lake (Taal, Laguna de Bay, etc.)

rendering_guidance: Render the funnel with the value cells EMPTY and the required-input labels filled. The honesty of the empty cells is the point. If a viewer asks 'where are the numbers', the answer — 'we need the BFAR operator counts and here is exactly why we will not guess them' — is stronger than a fabricated figure.
